steps.title: Use the lice date
steps.content: Hwang et al. date clothing-exclusive lice to as early as 170 thousand years ago. Because that variant needed ample clothing-related niches to survive, regular clothing use must already have been widespread by then.

steps.title: Compare the timeline
steps.content: Choice A makes an unsupported claim about other hominids. Choice B says clothing began after 170 thousand years ago, which conflicts with the lice date. Choice D focuses on spread after 70 thousand years ago. The remaining choice matches the earlier date and the need for widespread clothing use. The answer is C.
